rulings.Case Title: CHARLES EVANS JR. ET AL VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Quad C Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment

rulings.Case Title: BILLY JONES ET AL VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing the Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to ask follow up questions about Bob Johnson, who Plaintiff testified made purchases with him at the Pacific Coast Building Supply Store. (Triplett Declaration, Ex. B at 667; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64. 81 [the court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered].) Defendant also did not submit the responses to its special interrogatories or request of production of documents with its moving papers, but attached the responses to Defendant Close Building Materials, Inc. (Triplett Declaration, Exs. I-L.) The Court did not consider the responses contained in Exhibits I-L of the Supplemental Amended Declaration of Gardiner McKleroy as they do not comply with the notice requirement under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2). The declaration of Richard Bergeron is further insufficient to shift the burden as it does not address whether there was a Pacific Supply store operating in the Bay Area between 1960 and 1962. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MINGRAM VS. RILEY POWER INC; ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2019 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Hoffman Construction Company of Oregon's Motion to Quash Service is continued to February 27, 2020, pursuant to Plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery, which is GRANTED. The discovery is limited to the issue of personal jurisdiction. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by February 14, 2020. Supplemental reply, if any, due by February 21, 2020. The Court did not consider Defendant's reply papers due to untimely filing under Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
